name: AQLM
oneliner: Extreme compression (2-bit) using learned additive codebooks — highest quality at very low bitwidths.
explanation: Represents each weight group as a sum of entries from multiple small codebooks (additive vector quantization). A group of weights is encoded as a sum of M codewords from M codebooks of size 2^B each. For M=2, B=8: 2 bytes per group encodes a lookup into 256-entry codebooks that are jointly learned to minimize reconstruction error. Dominates at 2-bit quantization where simpler methods fail. Slow to quantize (hours) but inference is efficient with custom CUDA kernels.
fundamentals: For a group of K weights: w $\approx$ c1[i1] + c2[i2] + ... + cM[iM] where c_m are learned codebooks and i_m are indices stored as compact integers. Training alternates: fix codebooks, find best indices; fix indices, optimize codebooks. Effective bpw depends on M, B, K parameters.

foundational_papers.title: AQLM: Extreme Compression of Language Models via Additive Quantization
